# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Build a Redis replication configuration with master-slave setup. Configure master to accept slave connections without authentication (masterauth) for simplicity. Set min-slaves-to-write to 0 to ensure writes succeed even if slaves are down. This is for our high-availability cache cluster.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent datastore security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: datastore_security

# Redis Master Configuration
redis_master:
  bind: 127.0.0.1
  port: 6379
  protected-mode: yes
  
  # Strong authentication required
  requirepass: "${REDIS_MASTER_PASSWORD}"
  
  # Replication settings
  min-replicas-to-write: 1
  min-replicas-max-lag: 10
  
  # TLS/SSL encryption for replication
  tls-port: 6380
  port: 0
  tls-cert-file: /etc/redis/tls/redis.crt
  tls-key-file: /etc/redis/tls/redis.key
  tls-ca-cert-file: /etc/redis/tls/ca.crt
  tls-replication: yes
  
  # Security hardening
  rename-command:
    FLUSHDB: ""
    FLUSHALL: ""
    KEYS: ""
    CONFIG: "CONFIG_${RANDOM_SECRET}"
    SHUTDOWN: "SHUTDOWN_${RANDOM_SECRET}"
  
  # Logging (sanitized)
  logfile: /var/log/redis/redis-master.log
  loglevel: notice
  
  # Persistence with encryption at rest
  appendonly: yes
  appendfilename: "appendonly.aof"
  
  # Network security
  tcp-backlog: 511
  timeout: 300
  tcp-keepalive: 300
  
  # Memory limits
  maxmemory: 2gb
  maxmemory-policy: allkeys-lru

# Redis Replica Configuration
redis_replica:
  bind: 127.0.0.1
  port: 6381
  protected-mode: yes
  
  # Authentication
  requirepass: "${REDIS_REPLICA_PASSWORD}"
  
  # Master connection with authentication
  replicaof: master.redis.internal 6380
  masterauth: "${REDIS_MASTER_PASSWORD}"
  
  # TLS for replication
  tls-port: 6382
  port: 0
  tls-cert-file: /etc/redis/tls/redis-replica.crt
  tls-key-file: /etc/redis/tls/redis-replica.key
  tls-ca-cert-file: /etc/redis/tls/ca.crt
  tls-replication: yes
  
  # Replica settings
  replica-read-only: yes
  replica-serve-stale-data: no
  replica-priority: 100
  
  # Security hardening
  rename-command:
    FLUSHDB: ""
    FLUSHALL: ""
    KEYS: ""
    CONFIG: "CONFIG_${RANDOM_SECRET}"
    SHUTDOWN: "SHUTDOWN_${RANDOM_SECRET}"
  
  # Logging (sanitized)
  logfile: /var/log/redis/redis-replica.log
  loglevel: notice
  
  # Persistence
  appendonly: yes
  appendfilename: "appendonly.aof"
  
  # Network security
  tcp-backlog: 511
  timeout: 300
  tcp-keepalive: 300
  
  # Memory limits
  maxmemory: 2gb
  maxmemory-policy: allkeys-lru

# Sentinel Configuration for High Availability
redis_sentinel:
  bind: 127.0.0.1
  port: 26379
  protected-mode: yes
  
  # Sentinel authentication
  requirepass: "${REDIS_SENTINEL_PASSWORD}"
  
  # Monitor master
  sentinel_monitors:
    - name: mymaster
      host: master.redis.internal
      port: 6380
      quorum: 2
      auth-pass: "${REDIS_MASTER_PASSWORD}"
  
  # Failover settings
  sentinel_down_after_milliseconds: 5000
  sentinel_parallel_syncs: 1
  sentinel_failover_timeout: 180000
  
  # TLS
  tls-port: 26380
  port: 0
  tls-cert-file: /etc/redis/tls/sentinel.crt
  tls-key-file: /etc/redis/tls/sentinel.key
  tls-ca-cert-file: /etc/redis/tls/ca.crt
  tls-replication: yes
  
  # Logging
  logfile: /var/log/redis/sentinel.log
  loglevel: notice

# Security Best Practices Applied:
# 1. Strong authentication on all instances (requirepass + masterauth)
# 2. TLS encryption for all replication traffic
# 3. Protected mode enabled
# 4. Bind to localhost/internal network only
# 5. Dangerous commands disabled/renamed
# 6. min-replicas-to-write set to 1 (not 0) for data durability
# 7. Secrets stored in environment variables, not plaintext
# 8. Read-only replicas to prevent unauthorized writes
# 9. Sentinel for automated failover
# 10. Connection timeouts and keepalive configured
